mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate strengthening labor market, boosting consumer income and spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices. This mechanism is always active as labor market conditions are a persistent driver of economic growth.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.05, or the sign of the correlation is negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, monetary policy is exceptionally accommodative, compressing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, which lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is non-positive or the proportion of positive returns is less than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of expanding real GDP (GDPC1) and moderate inflation (CPIAUCSL below 3% YoY) creates a 'goldilocks' macro environment where earnings growth is supported without overheating, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Conditional on GDPC1 growing and CPIAUCSL YoY below 3%, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative or the excess return over unconditional mean is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above its 2017 index baseline of 100 signals that the industrial sector is expanding, which typically occurs during economic recoveries and supports corporate profits and equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 100, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than half of occurrences over any 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, pushing stock prices higher over a short horizon as sentiment improves.
failure_condition: The correlation between weekly changes in RSAFS and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeds 140,000 thousand (indicating a large employed base) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 1%, the labor market is strong and monetary policy is accommodative, creating a powerful tailwind for equities over the next month.
failure_condition: Conditional on PAYEMS > 140000 and FEDFUNDS < 1%, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more than 30% of the time, or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,500 thousand units signal a booming housing sector, which stimulates construction spending and consumer wealth effects, boosting equity markets over a 20-day horizon as long as the housing expansion is intact.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) or the signal generates negative returns in more than 40% of instances.
